title: ophrys oestrifera subsp. rhodostephane
intro: Ophrys oestrifera subsp. rhodostephane, also known as the Fly Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that belongs to the genus Ophrys. This plant is known for its unique appearance, which mimics the female fly in terms of color and shape, attracting male flies for pollination. The medicinal properties of the Fly Orchid include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds and reduce swelling. In herbal preparations, the Fly Orchid is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments that promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve overall health.
